title: Seat harness buckle
abstract: A buckle for a seat harness, comprising: â¢ a clasp and at least one detachable strap anchor for detachably securing a harness strap to the clasp, the clasp and the detachable strap anchor having complementary formations that engage to secure the strap anchored to the clasp and are able to be disengaged to release the strap anchor from the clasp, and â¢ a release actuator mounted to the clasp for disengaging the complementary formations; wherein the release actuator is movable relative to the clasp in such manner that a predetermined sequence of at least two movements in different directions must be performed in a specified order to disengage the complementary formations.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: A buckle for a seat harness, comprising: â¢ a clasp and at least one detachable strap anchor for detachably securing a harness strap to the clasp, the clasp and the detachable strap anchor having complementary formations that engage to secure the strap anchored to the clasp and are able to be disengaged to release the strap anchor from the clasp, and â¢ a release actuator mounted to the clasp for disengaging the complementary formations; wherein the release actuator is movable relative to the clasp in such manner that a predetermined sequence of at least two movements in different directions must be performed in a specified order to disengage the complementary formations.. A buckle according to claim 1 , further comprising biasing means arranged such that each movement in the predetermined sequence is against a bias. A buckle according to claim 2, wherein the complementary formations comprise a fixed snib and a flexible hasp with a hasp aperture to receive the snib for mutual engagement, and wherein the release actuator comprises means for flexing the hasp to remove the snib from the hasp aperture during a final movement in the predetermined sequence of at least two movements. A buckle according to claim 3, wherein the clasp has a ring-shaped body with a central cavity in which the release actuator is movably mounted and with at least one side aperture for receiving the hasp of the at least one strap anchor, and wherein the snib is fixed to the ring shaped body proximate the side aperture and the hasp elastically flexes during sliding contact with the snib during insertion into the side aperture, until the hasp aperture is biased onto the snib to secure the strap anchor. 5. A buckle according to claim 4, wherein the clasp has a back plate closing the central cavity on one face of the ring shaped body while the release actuator closes the central cavity on the opposing face, the back plate having an abutment for alignment with a protruding surface of the release actuator to prevent disengagement of the hasp from the snib, the release actuator being biased to a position in which the abutment aligns with the protruding surface. 6. A buckle according to claim 5, wherein the predetermined sequence has two movements, the first movement being to slide the protruding surface out of alignment with the abutment on the back plate, and the second movement being a depression of the release actuator towards the back plate to disengage the snib and the hasp. 7. A buckle according to claim 6, wherein the release actuator is a button biased away from the back plate. 8. A buckle according to claim 7, wherein the buckle has two strap anchors that releasably attach to opposing sides of the clasp through respective side apertures, and two secondary strap anchors which also releasably attach harness straps to the buckle. 9. A buckle according to claim 8, wherein the two strap anchors are main strap anchors and the secondary strap anchors each releasably engage one of the main strap anchors, respectively, as well as the clasp such that disengaging the main strap anchors from the clasp simultaneously allows the secondary strap anchors to disengage from the clasp. 10. A buckle according to claim 9, wherein the main strap anchors and the secondary strap anchors each have a strap threading hole. 1 1 . A buckle according to claim 10, wherein the clasp main body has a strap threading hole, the buckle arranged to provide for a 5 point harness. 12. A buckle according to claim 7, wherein the biasing means comprise two coil springs, one for providing a bias against sliding movement of the release button and the other providing a bias against the depression of the release button. 13. A buckle according to claim 1 , wherein the clasp main body and strap anchors are made up of injection moulded polymer components.
